content: Ah! `code.gs` line 19 returns `HtmlService.createHtmlOutputFromFile("Index")`! Wait, in Google Apps Script, `HtmlService.createHtmlOutputFromFile("Index")` loads the file named `Index.html`! And where is `Index.html` located? Wait! In Google Apps Script, all tĂ¡Â»â€¡p are in the same flat folder, so the file name is just `Index` (which is `Index.html` in our repo). Let's see what `Index.html` files are in our repository: We saw: - `d:\QC_APP\Index_GAS.html` (size: 161,585 bytes) - `d:\QC_APP\placeholders\Index.html` (size: 161,585 bytes) Wait! Why is there a `placeholders/Index.html` and `Index_GAS.html`? Let's look at `compile_index.ps1` again. Line 5: `$outputPathIndexPlaceholders = "d:\QC_APP\placeholders\Index.html"` Line 6: `$outputPathIndexRoot = "d:\QC_APP\Index_GAS.html"` And: Line 77: `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($outputPathIndexPlaceholders, $compiledContent, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)` Line 78: `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($outputPathIndexRoot, $compiledContent, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)` Wait! The script `compile_index.ps1` compiles `fabric_testing.html` into `Index.html` inside placeholders, and `Index_GAS.html` inside the root! But wait, what about the main app `index.html` (which is the Portal that wraps everything, as seen in line 166: ``)? When the user clicks "Fabric Testing" in the sidebar of `index.html`, what module does it load? Let's check `index.html` line 83: ```html 83: 84: 85: Fabric Testing 86: ``` And in `app.js` line 28: ```javascript 'materials_approval': { title: 'FABRIC TESTING LOGS', url: 'placeholders/fabric_testing.html' }, ``` So it loads `placeholders/fabric_testing.html`! But wait! If the user says: "sao fabric testing bĂ¡Â»â€¹ lĂ¡Â»â€”i trĂ¡Â»â€˜ng trĂ†Â¡n vĂ¡ÂºÂ­y nĂƒÂ³ nĂ¡ÂºÂ±m trong app chĂ¡Â»Â© khĂƒÂ´ng nĂ¡ÂºÂ±m riĂƒÂªng lĂ¡ÂºÂ» bĂƒÂªn ngoĂƒÂ i nhĂƒÂ©" Wait, does it mean they ran the app inside Google Sheets, and the Google Sheets app serves `Index.html` or `Index_GAS.html`? Ah! If they are running the Google Apps Script web app, the web app is served by `code.gs`. Let's check what `code.gs` serves. Wait, let's look at `code.gs` in the root! Let's view `d:\QC_APP\code.gs`. This will tell us what file GAS loads when the user opens the web app.
